Mount Everest Reaching an elevation of 29,032 feet (8,849 meters), Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world. # Mount Everest. Mount Everest is on the crest of the Great Himalayas of southern Asia. Its most common Tibetan name, Chomolungma, means “Goddess Mother of the World” or “Goddess of the Valley.” The Sanskrit name Sagarmatha means literally “Peak of Heaven.” Mount Everest was also previously referred to as Peak XV; it was renamed for Sir George Everest in 1865. Reaching an elevation of 29,032 feet (8,849 meters), Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.
